steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The previous sentence claims Chaucer pioneered literary use of English. The sentence with the blank provides documented evidence (2,000+ first-used words) that confirms and strengthens that claim.

steps.title: Match the transition
steps.content:
The logical relationship is confirmation: sentence 2 backs up sentence 1 with concrete evidence.
  • Choice A — "However,": signals contrast or an exception to what came before. The evidence does not contradict the pioneering claim; it supports it. ✗
  • Choice B — "Besides,": signals an additional, separate point. The 2,000+ words are not a separate point but direct evidence for the same claim. ✗
  • Choice C — "In fact,": signals confirmation with stronger or more specific evidence, often adding emphasis. The documented word count proves the pioneering claim. ✓
  • Choice D — "On the contrary,": signals direct contradiction of the prior sentence. The evidence agrees with the claim, not contradicts it. ✗
That matches choice C — C is correct.
